title: unifmes
text: unifmes Get the text of a Uniface error message. Explain this code int unifmes (unsigned char*Buffer); Copy Parameters Buffer The message (Output) Return Values The number of bytes returned (not including the zero terminator) Use Library: yrtl Description unifmes copies the text of the Uniface start-up error message, which would normally be displayed on standard output or the transcript window, to the specified buffer. This function can be called from a 3GL call-in application when the call to unifbeg has failed, so it can display the error message. buffer should be large enough to hold a typical Uniface start-up error message (200 bytes is a safe size). The following call-in program starts the Uniface environment with unifbeg , deliberately asking for a non-existing application shell to cause it to fail, then uses unifmes to print the exact error message. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> void main (void) { char buf[200]; usetparm(0, "a_nonexisting_startup_shell.aps", ""); unifbeg(0); unifmes(buf); printf(msg, "unifbeg() failed, error %d (%s)\n", ret, buf); } Copy Related Links unifbeg Start an application, or initialize the Uniface environment, so forms can be executed.
